content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Tom ___ (eat) a big sandwich for lunch yesterday.
distractor_rationale: present simple cannot describe a completed action in the past with 'yesterday'
past participle alone cannot form a simple past statement without an auxiliary
invented regular form; 'eat' is irregular and its past simple is 'ate', not 'eatted'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Maria ___ (walk) to school last Monday.
distractor_rationale: present simple cannot refer to a completed action with 'last Monday'
present participle alone cannot form a past tense statement
base form cannot be used as a past simple without an auxiliary verb

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Anna ___ (buy) a new bag at the market last weekend.
distractor_rationale: incorrect regular form; 'buy' is irregular and its past simple is 'bought', not 'buyed'
present simple cannot describe a completed action with 'last weekend'
present continuous cannot describe a completed single action in the past

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: James ___ (not / go) to the party last night because he felt very tired.
distractor_rationale: double past marking is incorrect; after 'didn't' the verb must stay in the base form
present tense auxiliary 'doesn't' cannot describe a past event with 'last night'
negation without an auxiliary verb is not grammatically possible in English past simple

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Sara ___ (finish) all her homework two hours ago and then watched TV.
distractor_rationale: base form cannot be used as a past simple; a regular verb needs the -ed ending
present simple cannot describe a completed action with 'two hours ago'
present perfect cannot be used with the specific past time marker 'two hours ago'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: ___ David (call) his mother last night after he got home from work?
distractor_rationale: inverting the main verb without 'did' is not grammatically correct in past simple questions
present tense auxiliary 'does' cannot form a past simple question with 'last night'
'was' is used for past continuous questions, not past simple questions with action verbs

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: We ___ (see) a really good film at the cinema last Saturday.
distractor_rationale: invented regular form; 'see' is irregular and its past simple is 'saw', not 'seed'
past participle alone cannot form a past simple statement without an auxiliary
base form cannot be used as a past simple for an irregular verb

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Lisa ___ (not / clean) her room yesterday, so her mother was very unhappy.
distractor_rationale: double past marking is incorrect; after 'didn't' the verb must remain in the base form
negation without the auxiliary 'did' is not grammatically possible in English past simple
present tense auxiliary 'doesn't' cannot describe a past situation with 'yesterday'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Mark ___ (think) the test was easy, so he handed in his paper after only ten minutes.
distractor_rationale: incorrect regular form; 'think' is irregular and its past simple is 'thought', not 'thinked'
present simple cannot describe a completed mental state during a past event
present continuous cannot describe a completed thought at a specific past moment

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The children ___ (play) outside for an hour, but then it started to rain heavily.
distractor_rationale: base form cannot be used as a past simple; a regular verb needs the -ed ending
past continuous describes an action that was interrupted, but the sentence presents two completed sequential events, not an interruption
present perfect cannot be used with a specific completed past time frame indicated by the sequence of events

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Tom ___ (walk) to school yesterday because the bus was late.
distractor_rationale: present simple cannot describe a completed action that happened yesterday
bare infinitive without auxiliary is not a valid finite verb form here
present participle alone cannot function as a main verb in a past statement

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: We ___ (not / have) any food at home last night, so we ordered a pizza.
distractor_rationale: present simple cannot express a completed past state from last night
double past marking is incorrect — 'didn't' already marks the past; the main verb must stay in its base form
negation without an auxiliary is not grammatically possible in standard English past simple

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Anna ___ (buy) a new jacket at the market last Saturday.
distractor_rationale: buy is an irregular verb — adding -ed is a common learner error that produces a non-existent form
present simple cannot describe a single completed purchase that happened last Saturday
present perfect cannot be used with the specific past time marker 'last Saturday'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Mark ___ (think) the exam was easy, so he finished it thirty minutes early.
distractor_rationale: think is an irregular verb — adding -ed produces a non-existent form that learners commonly over-apply
present simple cannot describe a completed mental state during a past event
past continuous implies an ongoing interrupted action, but the sentence describes a completed assessment followed by a finished result, not an interruption

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Lisa ___ (not / go) to the party last weekend because she felt very tired.
distractor_rationale: double past marking is incorrect — 'didn't' already carries the past tense; the main verb must remain in its base form, not the irregular past form
present simple cannot express a completed decision that took place last weekend
negation without the auxiliary 'did' is not grammatically acceptable in English past simple negative constructions

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom ___ his lunch at noon yesterday.
distractor_rationale: present simple cannot describe a completed action at a specific past time (noon yesterday)
bare infinitive is used in questions and negatives with did, not in affirmative past statements
eated is a common learner error applying regular -ed to an irregular verb

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She ___ to school by bus last Monday.
distractor_rationale: present simple cannot describe a completed action on a specific past day (last Monday)
goed is a very common learner error applying regular -ed to the irregular verb go
bare infinitive is used with did in questions and negatives, not in affirmative past statements

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: We ___ a new TV at the shop last Saturday.
distractor_rationale: bare infinitive cannot be used in an affirmative past simple statement without an auxiliary
buyed is a common learner error applying regular -ed to the irregular verb buy
present simple third-person singular cannot describe a completed past action

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The children ___ in the park for two hours yesterday afternoon.
distractor_rationale: bare infinitive cannot be used in an affirmative past simple statement
present simple third-person plural cannot describe a completed past action
present continuous cannot describe a completed action at a specific past time

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna ___ her keys on the table when she came home last night.
distractor_rationale: present simple cannot describe a completed action at a specific past time
putted is a learner error applying regular -ed to the irregular verb put, which has the same base and past form
present participle cannot be used alone as the main verb in a past simple statement

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Mark ___ finish his project on time because the computer stopped working.
distractor_rationale: doesn't is present simple negation and cannot describe a past event
didn't finished is a double past marking error — the auxiliary did already marks past tense, so the main verb must be bare infinitive
not alone cannot form a grammatical negative without an auxiliary verb

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: ___ Lisa call you last night, or did she send a message?
distractor_rationale: does is used to form present simple questions, not past simple questions
was forms past continuous or past be-questions, not past simple questions with action verbs
is forms present simple be-questions, not past simple questions

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: James ___ his bag and left the room quickly.
distractor_rationale: present simple cannot describe a completed past action in a narrative sequence
taked is a common learner error applying regular -ed to the irregular verb take
present participle cannot function as the main verb in a past simple affirmative sentence

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My parents ___ a letter to their friend in another city two days ago.
distractor_rationale: bare infinitive cannot be used in an affirmative past simple statement without an auxiliary
writed is a learner error applying regular -ed to the irregular verb write
present simple third-person plural form cannot describe a completed past action

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Sara ___ go to the market this morning because it was closed.
distractor_rationale: doesn't is present simple negation and cannot describe a past event
didn't went is a double past marking error — the auxiliary did already marks past tense, so go must remain in its base form
not alone cannot form a grammatical negative without an auxiliary verb
